visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
As per the update received from Portugal Embassy, they need all the official documents from Iran Government, legalized by Portugal Embassy in Tehran after Ministry of Foreign affairs (MoFA) and Justice Administrator stamp and sign (Police Clearances, Marriages, Academic certificates, birth certificates, etc..).

## Visa Fees

[Table:Family Reunification]

[Table:Family Reunification]

[Table:Family Reunification]
* Direct descendants of residence permit holders, in the framework of the family reunification legal provisions. Spouses pay fee.

## Documents Required

- Please note that your bank statement and account balance must include the exact amount in EUR / USD in addition to the IRR. Documents without the amount in EUR / USD will not be accepted.
Please click here to know more about the documents required 

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will
                                                                                        be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is approximately 90 days, unless otherwise stipulated by the Portugal Embassy depending on the supporting documents submitted.

## Download Form
Click here (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/national-visas/general-information/forms) for filing a visa application form.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will
                                                                                        be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children 
MUST be glued to the application
                                                                    form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for an application at the Embassy of Portugal is a minimum of 20 calendar days. Please note that all those applying for a visa for Portugal need to have at least
                                                                            20 days between the date of application and date of travel. Applications will not be accepted if this requirement is not adhered to. 
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

visa_types.name: Short Term (C – Schengen)
visa_types.overview:
All applications have to be made in person by the applicant due to the biometric enrolment process.
All applicants must fully comply with relevant documentation required for respective visa categories. Incomplete applications may not be accepted.
The processing time for an application at the Embassy of Portugal is a minimum of 30 calendar days. Please note that all those applying for a visa for Portugal need to have at least 30 days between the date of application and date of travel. Applications will not be accepted if this requirement is not adhered to.
You may submit your visa application at the nearest VFS Global Portugal Visa Application Centre. Those applying for short-stay Schengen visas (for business, tourism, leisure) are advised to submit their application at the Portugal Visa Application Centre.
The Convention implementing the Schengen Agreement came into force on 26 March 1995. The Schengen countries require visitors to obtain a common visa, which permits entry to the Schengen Area. The Schengen countries have reached agreements with each other on entry requirements and a common visa application form.
As per the update received from Portugal Embassy, they need all the official documents from Iran Government, legalized by Portugal Embassy in Tehran after Ministry of Foreign affairs (MoFA) and Justice Administrator stamp and sign (Birth certificates, Title deed, Lease contract).

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will
                                                                                        be shown).

MUST be glued to the application
                                                                    form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
